text.title: Product Hunt Assets, Common Mistakes, and Agent Guardrails
text.summary: Tagline formula, gallery structure, common launch failure modes, and agent production constraints.

rule.statement: Tagline formula: [Action verb] [outcome] [for whom] [differentiator]. Under 60 characters. Validate length after generation.
rule.rationale: Action verbs signal what the product does immediately. Outcome communicates value. "For whom" targets the right audience. Under 60 characters prevents truncation in the feed.

example.note: Tagline: "Build beautiful landing pages in minutes" — action verb (Build), outcome (beautiful landing pages), timeframe as differentiator (in minutes). 43 characters.
Tagline: "AI-Powered Landing Page Generator for Modern Businesses" — no action verb for the user, "AI-Powered" is jargon, too long (55 chars but generic). Does not communicate user outcome.

list.item: Image 1: Hero shot with tagline overlay
Images 2-4: Key features with one-sentence explanations
Image 5: Testimonials or social proof numbers

rule.statement: Do not mention unfinished features in the listing or comments. PH commenters will test everything mentioned; negative comments about missing features compound quickly.
rule.rationale: PH comments are permanent and indexed. Overpromising on launch day creates immediate negative social proof that follows the product indefinitely.

antipattern.description: Launching without a pre-built email list or community.
antipattern.reason: Without warm contacts to drive early upvote velocity, the listing never reaches the first page. Building the list before launch is more important than the launch date itself.

antipattern.description: Launching on a weekend or during a major tech event.
antipattern.reason: Weekends have fewer active PH users; major events dominate attention. Tuesdays through Thursdays have the highest active user counts and lowest competition.

antipattern.description: First comment drafted as corporate marketing copy.
antipattern.reason: PH community values authenticity. A polished, corporate first comment reduces maker credibility. Write in first-person casual voice and review for authenticity before posting.

section.title: Agent Guardrails

rule.statement: Agent must never include "upvote us" or "share with your network" in any launch copy. Explicitly forbid these phrases in the system prompt.
rule.rationale: Asking for upvotes directly is a PH TOS violation and, if flagged, can result in removal of the listing. Let the product quality drive upvotes organically.

rule.statement: Agent drafts comment responses; a human must review and post them. No automated comment posting on public platforms.
rule.rationale: Real-time public responses require human judgment on tone, accuracy, and appropriateness. Agent responses risk misrepresenting the product or making commitments the team cannot keep.

section.reference: First comment template: maker intro, differentiators, special offer, roadmap teaser
Agent prompt: 5 tagline variants + first comment + launch email from product brief
